value.found_quote: Containers provide isolation through Linux namespaces. Each container has its own view of the filesystem, process tree, and network stack, while sharing the host kernel.
value.rationale: The page describes container-based isolation in detail, covering process isolation (own process tree, --pids-limit, --user non-root), resource limits (--memory, --cpus, --pids-limit), network control (--network none, proxy/Unix socket allowlists), and an ephemeral filesystem (--read-only root with --tmpfs that's "cleared when the container stops"). This substantiates all aspects of the claim.
